extraction.one_line_summary: CHEEK CORNY Giulio won from 21-rider breakaway while NIBALI attacked with 6.5km remaining on Mortirolo, exploding ROGLIC who lost 1:23 and dropped to third overall
tactical_patterns: Nibali attacked immediately at Mortirolo base (6.5km from summit) before gradient steepened, creating separation before rivals could respond.
Roglic isolated without teammates on Mortirolo while Yates maintained two-man support, revealing critical team depth difference on hardest climbs.
MAL OMA advanced position despite not attacking, benefiting from Nibali forcing selection while conserving energy for later stages.
21-rider breakaway contained multiple GC threats, forcing favorites to chase themselves rather than relying on team structure.

rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: positive: initiated decisive attack at Mortirolo base, dropped Roglic permanently, finished 4th on stage gaining substantial GC ground
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: suffered catastrophic isolation on Mortirolo, lost 1:23 and dropped from 2nd to 3rd GC, showed first major weakness
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: held pink jersey without teammates doing work, maintained position while Nibali attacked and Roglic cracked
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: maintained two-teammate support on Mortirolo while rivals isolated, finished in lead group despite not attacking personally
rider_signals.MAL OMA Bauke: positive: moved up GC without attacking, tactically smart to gain time while Nibali forced selection
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: neutral: dropped initially then recovered to lead group, erratic positioning but salvaged time gains on others
rider_signals.HIRT Jan: positive: challenged for stage win from breakaway, never won Grand Tour stage before, showed sprint strength
